An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four - in this episode, friends Kevin, Dave, Alan and Ken - take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thrilling new railway adventure following the Silk Road through the most populous country of Central Asia, Uzbekistan.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Rural treasures stolen and livestock lost - police uncover crimes beneath the soil.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists head to the beach to try to find a daughter's lost bracelet, given to her by her loving foster parents. A 160-year-old find leads archive detective Sam on a fascinating trail, which involves the author of Sherlock Holmes, and crafter Sally is inspired by the history of a Brown Betty teapot fished out of the lake - reinventing its story in her modern twist on its tale.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: See Hear explores how technology is transforming life for deaf people, from communication tools and AI to new ways of connecting, working and living in a digital world. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined in the studio by Billy-Jack Gerrard, who is passionate about AI and how it is shaping the future. He explains how AI works, how coding underpins everyday technology and how his own experiences as a deaf coder have influenced his AI career path and thinking.

programme.desc: Contestants compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions as four more contenders take the black chair under the spotlight. The specialist subjects are the history of the Orkney Islands, the films of Laurel and Hardy, Elizabeth David and Sir Garfield Sobers.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team visit Bristol to help organise a busy and cluttered home. The family, which includes three children under five, is struggling to find space for all their bikes, and the unusual layout of their front room needs some clever thinking to maximise the space and storage.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Competition for food becomes fierce as lion numbers rise; a lioness risks her life to feed her five cubs; Xudum's newborn leopard cubs face a deadly threat.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At the National Gallery in Ottawa, a desk converted from a piano, Bird scarers and a 16th century emerald jewel.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, experts Charles Hanson and Anita Manning are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Charles is valuing a rare Eureka clock and Anita is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games

programme.desc: Marcus' mission is to discover the ultimate simple Provençal tomato recipe. After searching for inspiration in the local market, he whips up a simple and delicious Provençal tomato sauce that's traditional, versatile and easy.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers head to Somerset to transform sailors' mess decks at Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. They must turn them into vibrant morale-boosting spaces where the young recruits can cook, eat and socialise. Olympic champion and ex-servicewoman Dame Kelly Holmes joins Michelle to judge the rooms.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a look at veggies with Jason Manford, Ahir Shah, Holly Walsh and Alan Davies.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it's flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: Flog It!

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry takes a look at the bank card. This humble piece of plastic is often taken for granted, but it's the digital key to accessing cash and is packed full of extraordinary technological innovations.

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: By 2016, Elon Musk is moving fast with several new business ideas: building underground tunnels, creating chips that can be inserted into people's brains and attempting to build the biggest battery factory the world has ever seen.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.title: Children of the Blitz
programme.desc: During the Blitz, over two million British children were not evacuated, instead staying at home in towns and cities at risk of being bombed. Now, the survivors of the bombings tell their stories.
